Title: Differential innate immune signalling via Ca2+ sensor protein kinases

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Calcium-dependent protein kinase (CDPK/CPK); Ser/Thr protein kinase with EF-hand Ca2+-binding regulatory domain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-associated oxidative burst contributing to innate immunity against bacterial pathogens
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively promotes flg22-induced oxidative burst and defense-related metabolite/gene programs; loss contributes to reduced ROS and compromised immunity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPK4 was identified in a functional screen as one of the specific subgroup I CDPKs whose constitutively active form induced the flg22-responsive NHL10 reporter. 2. Together with CPK5, CPK6 and CPK11, CPK4 was required for full flg22 induction of early defense genes including PHI1, NHL10, PER62 and PER4. 3. Silencing of CPK4 in higher-order cpk backgrounds further reduced flg22-triggered ROS burst and defense outputs, indicating redundant positive function in innate immune signalling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutively active overexpression in protoplast reporter assay; VIGS-mediated knockdown in higher-order mutant background; qRT-PCR of target genes; ROS luminol assay; pathogen assay
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RBOHB (potential direct target inferred from related potato CDPKs, not directly validated here)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flg22 treatment; MAMP signalling; bacterial infection with Pst DC3000
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with CPK5/6/11; cpk4 null mutant was not obtained and function was assessed by VIGS knockdown; paper emphasizes defense metabolites broadly rather than a single nutrient-quality metabolite.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No apparent phenotype under normal growth conditions reported for higher-order mutants
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance broad-spectrum disease resistance through manipulation of early immune signalling, but redundancy suggests multi-gene engineering would be required
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves; active during early flg22 response
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S-derived promoter for effector constructs; NHL10 promoter for reporter

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Calcium-dependent protein kinase (CDPK/CPK); Ser/Thr protein kinase with EF-hand Ca2+-binding regulatory domain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-associated oxidative burst contributing to pathogen resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flg22-induced ROS production and activation of defense-related metabolite/gene pathways; cpk5-containing higher-order mutants show reduced oxidative burst and increased bacterial susceptibility
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPK5 was a major positive CDPK identified by gain-of-function screening for activation of the flg22-responsive NHL10 promoter. 2. Transcriptome profiling with CPK5ac showed overlap with flg22-induced early genes, including genes involved in defense metabolites, cell wall and redox signalling. 3. Loss of CPK5 together with CPK6/11/4 progressively reduced flg22-triggered gene induction, ROS burst, and antibacterial immunity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutively active overexpression; ATH1 microarray profiling; T-DNA knockout; higher-order mutant analysis; qRT-PCR; ROS assay; bacterial growth assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flg22; other MAMPs; Pst DC3000 infection
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CPK5ac used as representative for the CPK5/6 pair in transcriptome profiling; functions independently of MAPK activation downstream of FLS2
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No apparent phenotype under normal growth conditions reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate positive immune regulator for crop engineering, but redundancy with related CDPKs suggests stacked manipulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-expressed; activated in early MAMP responses

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Calcium-dependent protein kinase (CDPK/CPK); Ser/Thr protein kinase with EF-hand Ca2+-binding regulatory domain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense oxidative burst in innate immunity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of flg22-induced ROS and defense-associated metabolic/gene outputs
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPK6 belonged to the same responsive subgroup I clade as CPK4/5/11 identified in the functional screen. 2. Genetic analysis showed CPK6 acts redundantly with CPK5, CPK11 and CPK4 to support induction of several flg22-responsive defense genes and oxidative burst. 3. CPK5 and CPK6 were specifically essential for full flg22 activation of CYP82C2.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutively active overexpression screen; T-DNA knockout; higher-order mutant analysis; qRT-PCR; ROS assay; pathogen assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flg22; MAMP signalling; Pst DC3000 infection
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly; not profiled separately by microarray because CPK5 represented the CPK5/6 pair
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No apparent phenotype under normal growth conditions reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as part of multi-CDPK engineering strategy for disease resistance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-expressed; active in early immune response

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Calcium-dependent protein kinase (CDPK/CPK); Ser/Thr protein kinase with EF-hand Ca2+-binding regulatory domain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense oxidative burst contributing to antibacterial immunity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of flg22-induced defense genes and ROS; contributes redundantly to antibacterial immunity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPK11 was identified as a subgroup I CDPK whose constitutively active form induced NHL10-LUC. 2. CPK11ac transcriptome profiling revealed many early flg22 target genes, including defense metabolite-, cell wall-, and redox-related genes, with some unique repressed targets. 3. Loss of CPK11 in higher-order mutants further impaired flg22-triggered gene induction, oxidative burst and pathogen defense.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutively active overexpression; ATH1 microarray profiling; T-DNA knockout; higher-order mutant analysis; qRT-PCR; ROS assay; pathogen assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: flg22; multiple MAMPs; Pst DC3000 infection
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CPK11ac but not CPK5ac also repressed some early flg22-responsive genes; functions independently of MAPK activation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No apparent phenotype under normal growth conditions reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of broad-spectrum immune engineering, especially in combination with related CDPKs
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-expressed; active in early MAMP response
